title: bridge-matrix: homeserver URL with non-standard port breaks bot MXID comparison
description: bridge-matrix/src/bridge.rs is_bot_sender: extracts domain from homeserver URL by stripping 'https://' then splitting on '/'. For 'https://matrix.example.com:8448/', this gives domain='matrix.example.com:8448', producing bot_mxid '@bot:matrix.example.com:8448'. That is a valid MXID and the comparison is correct. HOWEVER: if the admin configures matrix_homeserver='https://matrix.example.com:8448' (no trailing slash), split('/').next() gives 'matrix.example.com:8448', which is still correct. Edge case: 'https://matrix.example.com:8448/path' → domain='matrix.example.com:8448' which is correct. The real problem is that trim_start_matches('https://') on 'http://matrix.example.com' yields 'matrix.example.com' (stripping only one slash is not the issue — trim_start_matches strips the full prefix). But config validation rejects http://, so this is fine. No bug here on reflection.
close_reason: Not a bug: is_bot_sender handles ports correctly on analysis.
